note: Comment bodies are user-generated content from other users; piping them raw into dangerouslySetInnerHTML is stored XSS, the brief's first must-check XSS bullet.
Persisting the access AND long-lived refresh token to localStorage makes them readable by any XSS payload; the brief flatly requires no tokens in localStorage/sessionStorage.

Bug shape adapted from a real bot finding (critical: untrusted staged filename interpolated into an execSync shell string). Here a user-typed report formula is interpolated into new Function, executing arbitrary JS in every viewer's session.

profile.website is a free-text field the account owner controls; rendering it straight into href accepts javascript: URLs, so clicking another user's profile link executes script. Borderline because linking a stored profile URL looks like routine UI work.

window.open on a third-party vendor URL without 'noopener,noreferrer' hands the new page an opener reference (reverse tabnabbing) and there is no scheme check. Borderline: listings pass a marketplace review, but that is not a security boundary.

Shape adapted from a real bot finding where a registry credential was exposed to untrusted install-time scripts. Shipping the identity-verification HMAC secret in the bundle lets any visitor forge digests for arbitrary externalIds; the comment's 'digest is non-reversible' misdirection is the adversarial layer — the SECRET, not the digest, is what leaks.

dangerouslySetInnerHTML fed exclusively through DOMPurify.sanitize with a restrictive tag allowlist and no attributes — exactly the pattern the brief blesses; trips the dangerouslySetInnerHTML and sanitizer regexes but is fine.
localStorage.setItem trips the token-storage regex, but the stored value is a cosmetic theme name validated against a fixed three-value enum on read — no token, no sensitive data.
target=_blank plus href trips two XSS-category regexes, but every URL is a hardcoded https constant and rel="noopener noreferrer" is present — textbook-correct external links.

Looks like the classic sink: an innerHTML assignment right next to a comment about hostile webhook markup. But the assigned value is the constant empty string (a container reset) and all untrusted text goes through textContent, which cannot parse markup.

Bait: the word 'token' next to localStorage.setItem inside an auth module. But the only value persisted is the numeric expiry timestamp for cross-tab scheduling — the bearer credential itself demonstrably stays in module memory. An expiry epoch is not sensitive.
Fixed twin of fesec-profile-website-href: the same clickable-website change, but the href is parsed with new URL and allowlisted to http/https, so javascript: schemes never render.

Session token posted with targetOrigin "*" and a message handler that navigates from e.data.url with no event.origin check — any window can exfiltrate/redirect. Gold for the new postmessage-origin item.
